req.title: Create observation (boundary)
req.statement: A clinician shall create an Observation tied to a patient with LOINC-coded code element; Observations without code shall be rejected. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.
preconditions: role:clinician
patient exists

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates (1) successful creation of a LOINC-coded Observation tied to an existing patient, (2) rejection (HTTP 422) when the code element is absent, and (3) boundary behaviour on timestamps and numeric values. EP covers the valid/invalid code partition; NEG covers the missing-code error path; BVA covers extreme numeric values (0, max decimal) and ISO-8601 timestamp precision edges. DT is applied to capture the interaction between code-present/absent and value-present/absent. STC is applied to trace the Observation lifecycle from draft to final status. Each test POSTs to /Observation, cleans up via DELETE if a resource was created, and relies on shared fixtures for auth headers and a pre-existing patient ID.

tests.name: test_create_observation_valid_loinc_code
preconditions: Caller has role:clinician (Bearer token in <auth_headers> fixture)
A patient resource exists; its id is provided by <patient_id> fixture
LOINC code 8867-4 (heart rate) is a valid code in the system
actions: POST /Observation with resourceType, status='final', subject referencing patient, LOINC-coded code element, and a numeric valueQuantity
expected: Response body id is non-empty string
trace: R-HLT-030: observation stored when code present

tests.name: test_create_observation_missing_code_returns_422
preconditions: Caller has role:clinician (Bearer token in <auth_headers> fixture)
A patient resource exists; its id is provided by <patient_id> fixture
actions: POST /Observation with resourceType and subject but WITHOUT a code element
expected: Response body indicates validation failure related to missing code
trace: R-HLT-030: error pathway — 422 when code missing
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_create_observation_missing_code_returns_422(auth_headers, patient_id, base_url):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "valueQuantity": {
            "value": 72,
            "unit": "beats/min",
            "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org",
            "code": "/min"
        }
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Observation",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    raw = str(body).lower()
    assert "code" in raw or "required" in raw or "missing" in raw

tests.name: test_create_observation_boundary_numeric_zero_and_timestamp_precision
preconditions: Caller has role:clinician (Bearer token in <auth_headers> fixture)
A patient resource exists; its id is provided by <patient_id> fixture
LOINC code 2339-0 (glucose) is a valid code
actions: POST /Observation with numeric valueQuantity of exactly 0 (lower boundary)
Use effectiveDateTime with full ISO-8601 precision including milliseconds and UTC offset
expected: Response body effectiveDateTime is preserved and non-null
trace: R-HLT-030: boundary cases on numeric elements — value of 0
R-HLT-030: boundary cases on timestamps — millisecond precision

tests.name: test_create_observation_code_present_value_absent_decision
preconditions: Caller has role:clinician (Bearer token in <auth_headers> fixture)
A patient resource exists; its id is provided by <patient_id> fixture
LOINC code 55284-4 (blood pressure panel) supports value-less observations
actions: POST /Observation with code element present but no valueQuantity (code present / value absent decision column)
expected: HTTP 201 Created — code alone satisfies the code requirement
Response body contains no valueQuantity or valueQuantity is null
trace: R-HLT-030: decision table — code present, value absent → accepted
R-HLT-030: LOINC-coded code element required; value is optional

tests.name: test_observation_status_lifecycle_registered_to_final
preconditions: Caller has role:clinician (Bearer token in <auth_headers> fixture)
A patient resource exists; its id is provided by <patient_id> fixture
LOINC code 8310-5 (body temperature) is valid
Target system supports Observation status transitions: registered → preliminary → final
actions: POST /Observation with status='registered' and valid LOINC code
expected: First PUT returns HTTP 200 with status 'preliminary'
Second PUT returns HTTP 200 with status 'final'
trace: R-HLT-030: observation stored — verifies full lifecycle to final state
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_observation_status_lifecycle_registered_to_final(
    auth_headers, patient_id, base_url
):
    base_payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "code": {
            "coding": [{
                "system": "http://loinc.org",
                "code": "8310-5",
                "display": "Body temperature"
            }]
        },
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "valueQuantity": {"value": 37.0, "unit": "Cel",
                         "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org", "code": "Cel"}
    }
    r1 = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Observation",
                       json={**base_payload, "status": "registered"},
                       headers=auth_headers)
    assert r1.status_code == 201
    obs_id = r1.json()["id"]
    assert r1.json()["status"] == "registered"
    try:
        r2 = requests.put(f"{base_url}/Observation/{obs_id}",
                          json={**base_payload, "id": obs_id, "status": "preliminary"},
                          headers=auth_headers)
        assert r2.status_code == 200
        assert r2.json()["status"] == "preliminary"
        r3 = requests.put(f"{base_url}/Observation/{obs_id}",
                          json={**base_payload, "id": obs_id, "status": "final"},
                          headers=auth_headers)
        assert r3.status_code == 200
        assert r3.json()["status"] == "final"
    finally:
        requests.delete(f"{base_url}/Observation/{obs_id}", headers=auth_headers)

tests.name: test_create_observation_boundary_large_numeric_value
preconditions: Caller has role:clinician (Bearer token in <auth_headers> fixture)
A patient resource exists; its id is provided by <patient_id> fixture
LOINC code 2339-0 (glucose) is valid
actions: POST /Observation with valueQuantity.value set to 9999999.99 (upper practical boundary for a decimal numeric field)
trace: R-HLT-030: boundary cases on numeric elements — upper bound decimal
